rulings.Case Title: WEN-SHI YU VS. ROBERT MCCAFFERY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Anbd Motion For Substitution Of Personal Representative For Deceased Deft
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WEN-SHI YU'S Motion For Substitution Of Personal Representative For Deceased Defendant. Continued to April 1, 2014 to give Sean McCaffery, Executor for the Estate of Robert McCaffery, the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. McCaffery must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 19, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, IN THEIR CAPACITIES AS et al VS. SIED MEHRDAD ZARRINSARY, INDIVIDUALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, IN THEIR CAPACITIES AS, CARPENTERS VACATION-HOLIDAY TRUST FUND FOR, CARPENTERS PENSION TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, CARPENTERS ANNUITY TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, CARPENTERS TRAINING TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, NORTHERN CALIFORNIA CARPENTERS REGIONAL COUNCIL'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Granted. No opposition filed. The Court will award $700.00 in reasonable attorney fees and $834.54 in costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers, including the amounts above for reasonable attorney fees and costs. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARL HILSZ VS VERA CORT, et a
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And Her Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 19. 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CARL HILSZ'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And Her Attorneys Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff¿s motion is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $1,800 are awarded. The sanctions and the following documents are to be produced within 10 days of the order: 1. Parking lot diagrams¿Monthly diagrams from 1/1/07 to 1/1/14 (84 months less the 9 already produced.) 2. Leases¿All parking leases for all 103 spaces from 2007 to the present. 3. Payment records¿Checks showing all payment of taxes to the City from 2007 to the present. 4. Privilege Log¿The log should explain each redaction and the basis for the privilege asserted. 5. Accounting records¿These would include the book, legers, spreadsheets and other records which a business customarily keeps in their business operations showing the revenue and expenses of the business from 2007 to the present. 6. Quarterly tax returns¿There should have been 28 tax returns from 2007 to the present. Only 19 were produced. The rest are to be produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Response In Enforcement Of Order Of Pro Tem Judge Charles Geehart, Strike Objections And Legal Conculsions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S MOTION To Compel Further Response In Enforcement Of Order Of Pro Tem Judge Charles Geehart, Strike Objections And Legal Conculsions And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 34-49 in enforcement of order of Pro Tem Judge Charles Geehart is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is to provide more factual basis for their responses to these interrogatories within 10 days of this order. The objections already asserted are not stricken. Sanctions requested by the defendant are awarded in the amount of $1,500. Issue preclusion sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. ADI SIMHAYOF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That Matters In Req. For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC'S MOTION For Order That Matters In Req. For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Deft Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition was received. The plaintiff's motion is granted. The requests are deemed admitted. Sanctions in the amount of $332.50 are also granted and are to be paid by defendant within 10 days of the order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER STACK VS. RYAN CANSLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER and KELLY CORWELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for intentional tort if plaintiff can in good faith allege willful disregard of his rights. Accepting as true the allegations in the Complaint establishes only that Defendant illegally ran a red light and hit Plaintiff's vehicle; they do not establish Defendant's state of mind or intent, or of Defendant's awareness either that the light was red or of the likelihood of colliding with another vehicle even if he was aware. In addition, the third cause of action is vague in that it does not distinguish which "Defendant" is the subject of the description in para. IT-1. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER STACK VS. RYAN CANSLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER and KELLY CORWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges only that Defendant had a red light but failed to stop, proceeded into the intersection, and struck Plaintiff's vehicle. There is no allegation that Defendant intentionally failed to stop, that he knew the light was red but nonetheless proceeded through the intersection, or that he intended to strike Plaintiff's vehicle. The complaint lacks allegations of actual intent or facts establishing Defendant's awareness of dangerous consequences and a deliberate failure to avoid them. Moreover, the Complaint does not allege facts that establish despicable conduct on the part of Defendant(s). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GUY O. KORNBLUM VS. GENESIS SPECIALTY TILE & ASSOCIATES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Guy Kornblum'S Application For Right To Attach Order; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 18, PLAINTIFF GUY KORNBLUM'S Application For Right To Attach Order. Denied. Plaintiff has not met his initial burden of proof. (CCP § 484.090.) Although Plaintiff has stated facts to support his application (Application, 7c), there is no declaration showing personal knowledge or establishing foundation for the proffered facts. "Even if you check the third box, however, you must still support your factual allegations by way of declarations or affidavits." (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (Rutter) 9:906.1.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WESTERN VS. WOODBANK, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment; Notice
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF WESTERN PLYWOOD, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Application For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment; To be heard at 9:30 am on the Law and Motion calendar.

rulings.Case Title: WESTERN VS. WOODBANK, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Western Plywood, Inc. A California Corporation'S Application For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014, Line 19, PLAINTIFF WESTERN PLYWOOD, INC.'S Application For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment. Denied, without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to file a supporting memorandum with its application. (CRC 3.1113(a).) An application for right to attach order is not one of the enumerated exceptions to this rule. (CRC 3.1114(a).) Notwithstanding this procedural error, the motion is denied. Plaintiff admits that this action is subject to an arbitration agreement. (Brownstein Decl., 3; Woerner Decl., Exhibit A, 14.) In its application, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the award to which Plaintiff may be entitled to may be rendered ineffectual without provisional relief. (CCP § 1281.8(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
